item.title: ‘People Here Do Not Consider Themselves Poor. They Consider Themselves Broke.’
item.description: Big parts of the Rio Grande Valley turned against the party in recent years. One candidate is trying to win it back, one quinceañera at a time.

item.title: After a Hero’s Fall, What to Do With the Art That Honored Him?
item.description: Artists who created public depictions of the civil rights icon Cesar Chavez have had revisit their works after revelations emerged of Mr. Chavez’s sex...

item.title: The Generals Who Are Now Running Iran
item.description: The killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ushered in a new form of collective leadership in the country, with more power for the Revolutionary Guards.

item.title: $106 Billion Loan Reflects E.U.’s View That Peace in Ukraine Is Far Away
item.description: Unlike previous European assistance packages, this one is heavily weighted toward military spending, meant to put Ukraine on solid footing for a long ...

item.title: Russian Officials Say Town’s Air Is Toxic, Days After Strike on Oil Refinery
item.description: Since Ukraine attacked the refinery in Tuapse, starting a huge fire, residents have reported drops of dark, oily toxins falling from the sky.
